title: triticum subspontaneum
intro: Triticum subspontaneum, also known as wild wheat or wheatgrass, is a wild grass species that belongs to the wheat family and is native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Triticum subspontaneum include its ability to boost the immune system, lower blood pressure, and improve digestion, as well as its potential to prevent certain types of cancer. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed fresh or dried, depending on the specific application.
